title: Karva Chauth
intro: Karva Chauth is one of the most sacred Hindu festivals observed primarily by married women for the long life, good health, and well-being of their husbands.
description:
Karva Chauth is one of the most sacred Hindu festivals observed primarily by married women for the long life, good health, and well-being of their husbands. The festival falls on the Chaturthi (fourth day) of the Krishna Paksha in the Hindu month of Kartik, usually in October or November. On this day, women observe a strict Nirjala Vrat (fast without food and water) from sunrise until moonrise and perform Karva Chauth Puja with devotion.

According to Hindu traditions, Karva Chauth is associated with the legends of Queen Veervati, Karva, and the divine blessings of Lord Shiva and Goddess Parvati. The festival symbolizes love, devotion, trust, and the sacred bond of marriage. It is believed that observing this fast with sincerity brings prosperity, marital happiness, and Akhand Saubhagya (everlasting marital bliss).

Women dress in traditional attire, perform Solah Shringar, listen to the Karva Chauth Vrat Katha, offer Arghya to the Moon, and break their fast after seeing the Moon and their husband through a sieve. In modern times, many husbands also observe the fast as a symbol of mutual love and respect.

Festival Month

Kartik (October – November)

Significance / Importance

Karva Chauth holds great religious, cultural, and emotional significance in Hinduism. The festival represents the love, faith, commitment, and lifelong bond between husband and wife. Married women observe this fast and worship Lord Shiva, Goddess Parvati, Lord Ganesha, Lord Kartikeya, and Chandra Dev (the Moon God) for their husband’s long life, happiness, and prosperity.

It is believed that observing Karva Chauth with devotion brings marital harmony, good fortune, and divine blessings. The festival also encourages family unity, gratitude, and spiritual discipline. Today, Karva Chauth is celebrated across India and by Hindu communities worldwide as a beautiful expression of love and devotion.

Puja Vidhi

1. Wake up before sunrise and eat Sargi (where this tradition is followed).
2. Observe a Nirjala fast from sunrise until moonrise.
3. Clean and decorate the worship area in the evening.
4. Place idols or images of Lord Shiva, Goddess Parvati, Lord Ganesha, and Lord Kartikeya.
5. Listen to or read the Karva Chauth Vrat Katha.
6. Offer roli, rice, flowers, sweets, fruits, and the Karva during the puja.
7. Offer Arghya (water) to the Moon after moonrise.
8. View the Moon and then your husband through a sieve before breaking the fast.

Related Katha / Mantra / Aarti / Chalisa

* Karva Chauth Vrat Katha
* Shiva Mantra
* Parvati Mantra
* Ganesha Mantra
* Shiva Aarti
* Parvati Aarti
* Shiva Chalisa

faq.a1: Karva Chauth is observed on the Chaturthi of the Krishna Paksha in the Hindu month of Kartik.
faq.a2: It is celebrated for the long life, good health, happiness, and prosperity of the husband.
faq.q3: Who is worshipped on Karva Chauth?
faq.a3: Lord Shiva, Goddess Parvati, Lord Ganesha, Lord Kartikeya, and Chandra Dev are worshipped on this day.
faq.q4: In which month is Karva Chauth celebrated?
faq.a4: Karva Chauth usually falls in October or November.
faq.q5: When is the Karva Chauth fast broken?
faq.a5: The fast is broken after offering Arghya to the Moon and seeing the Moon and the husband through a sieve.
faq.q6: Why is the Karva Chauth Vrat Katha recited?
faq.a6: The Vrat Katha is recited to understand the significance of the fast and seek divine blessings for a happy married life.
faq.q7: Can unmarried women observe Karva Chauth?
faq.a7: Yes, in many traditions, unmarried women observe the fast to pray for a suitable life partner.
faq.q8: What is the spiritual significance of Karva Chauth?
faq.a8: Karva Chauth symbolizes love, devotion, faith, commitment, marital harmony, and the sacred bond between husband and wife.
